reasoning: Empty set is the identity for union, so mapping distributes correctly. Standard set algebra property.
Set union is commutative by definition. This doesn't even depend on the lemma under test.
`s + s == s` for sets (union is idempotent), so `(s + s).map(f) == s.map(f)` follows directly. Correct property.
`s1 ⊆ s1 ∪ s2` implies `s1.map(f) ⊆ (s1 ∪ s2).map(f)`. Standard monotonicity of set map over union.
